services.title: Our Services
services.subtitle: Comprehensive orthopedic treatments tailored to your needs.
services.pageSubtitle: From diagnosis through recovery, we offer a full spectrum of orthopedic services using the latest surgical techniques and evidence-based treatment protocols.
services.jointReplacementDesc: Advanced hip, knee, and shoulder replacement surgeries using the latest prosthetic technologies for lasting mobility.
services.sportsMedicineDesc: Specialized treatment for athletic injuries including ACL reconstruction, rotator cuff repair, and return-to-sport programs.
services.spineSurgeryDesc: Minimally invasive spinal procedures for herniated discs, spinal stenosis, and degenerative conditions.
services.arthroscopyDesc: Minimally invasive joint surgery using advanced camera technology for diagnosis and treatment of joint disorders.
services.fractureCareDesc: Expert treatment of simple and complex fractures with state-of-the-art fixation techniques and guided recovery.
services.rehabilitationDesc: Comprehensive post-operative and injury rehabilitation programs designed to optimize recovery and restore full function.
services.jointReplacementDetail: Joint replacement surgery, also known as arthroplasty, is one of the most successful procedures in modern medicine. We specialize in total and partial replacements of the hip, knee, and shoulder using the latest implant technologies, including ceramic, titanium, and cross-linked polyethylene components. Our approach emphasizes precise alignment through computer-assisted navigation and robotic-assisted techniques, ensuring optimal implant positioning for maximum longevity and natural movement. Each procedure is tailored to the patient's anatomy, activity level, and long-term goals.
services.jointReplacementConditions: Severe osteoarthritis of the hip, knee, or shoulder; Rheumatoid arthritis causing joint destruction; Avascular necrosis (bone death from loss of blood supply); Post-traumatic arthritis following fractures; Failed previous joint surgery or implant wear; Bone tumors affecting the joint; Significant joint deformity limiting daily activities
services.jointReplacementExpectations: Your journey begins with a comprehensive evaluation including advanced imaging and biomechanical assessment. We discuss all available options, from partial to total replacement, and select the optimal implant for your anatomy. Surgery is performed under regional or general anesthesia, typically lasting 1-2 hours. We utilize enhanced recovery protocols, meaning most patients begin walking within hours of surgery. Hospital stay is usually 1-2 days, with some patients eligible for outpatient surgery.
services.jointReplacementRecovery: Recovery follows a structured timeline. Weeks 1-2 focus on wound healing and gentle range-of-motion exercises with physical therapy beginning immediately. By weeks 3-6, most patients walk without assistive devices and resume light daily activities. Months 2-3 bring significant strength improvements and return to low-impact activities. Full recovery typically occurs by 3-6 months, with continued improvement up to one year. Modern implants are designed to last 20-30 years with proper care.
services.sportsMedicineDetail: Our sports medicine program combines surgical expertise with a deep understanding of athletic biomechanics to treat injuries at every level, from weekend warriors to professional athletes. We offer advanced arthroscopic and open surgical techniques for ligament reconstruction, tendon repair, and cartilage restoration. Our integrated approach includes pre-surgical optimization, state-of-the-art surgical intervention, and sport-specific rehabilitation protocols designed to return athletes to peak performance safely and efficiently.
services.sportsMedicineConditions: ACL, PCL, and multi-ligament knee injuries; Meniscus tears and cartilage damage; Rotator cuff tears and shoulder instability; Labral tears of the hip and shoulder; Tennis elbow and golfer's elbow; Achilles tendon injuries and ruptures; Stress fractures and overuse injuries; Patellar tendinopathy and runner's knee
services.sportsMedicineExpectations: Treatment begins with a thorough evaluation including sports-specific functional testing, MRI, and biomechanical analysis. We develop a personalized treatment plan that may include conservative management, regenerative therapies such as PRP injections, or surgical intervention. Arthroscopic procedures are performed through small incisions using high-definition cameras, minimizing tissue damage and accelerating recovery. We work closely with your athletic trainers and coaches to coordinate a seamless return-to-sport program.
services.sportsMedicineRecovery: Recovery is tailored to your sport and competitive timeline. Initial healing takes 1-2 weeks with protected movement and pain management. Weeks 2-6 focus on restoring range of motion and early strengthening. Months 2-4 involve progressive resistance training and sport-specific drills. Return to full sport participation typically occurs between 4-9 months depending on the procedure, guided by objective functional testing benchmarks rather than arbitrary timelines.
services.spineSurgeryDetail: Our spine surgery program addresses the full spectrum of spinal conditions using the most advanced minimally invasive techniques available. We employ microscopic and endoscopic approaches that preserve surrounding muscle and tissue, resulting in less pain, shorter hospital stays, and faster recovery compared to traditional open surgery. Our expertise spans the entire spine, from cervical to lumbar, and we utilize cutting-edge navigation systems and intraoperative imaging to ensure precision and safety throughout every procedure.
services.spineSurgeryConditions: Herniated or bulging discs causing nerve compression; Spinal stenosis (narrowing of the spinal canal); Degenerative disc disease; Spondylolisthesis (vertebral slippage); Spinal fractures from trauma or osteoporosis; Sciatica and radiculopathy; Cervical myelopathy; Adult scoliosis and spinal deformities
services.spineSurgeryExpectations: Evaluation includes a detailed neurological examination, advanced imaging (MRI, CT, X-rays), and sometimes diagnostic injections to precisely identify the pain source. Many spinal conditions respond well to conservative treatment, and we always explore non-surgical options first. When surgery is indicated, we discuss the specific technique, expected outcomes, and realistic timeline. Most minimally invasive spine procedures are performed through incisions smaller than one inch, often as outpatient or with a single overnight stay.
services.spineSurgeryRecovery: Minimally invasive approaches dramatically shorten recovery. Most patients walk within hours of surgery and return home the same day or next morning. Weeks 1-2 involve activity modification and gradual return to walking. Weeks 3-6 see progressive increase in daily activities and beginning of physical therapy. Most patients return to desk work within 2-4 weeks and physical labor within 6-12 weeks. Complete spinal fusion healing, when applicable, occurs over 3-6 months with ongoing strengthening.
services.arthroscopyDetail: Arthroscopic surgery represents the gold standard in minimally invasive joint diagnosis and treatment. Using a camera the diameter of a pencil and specialized micro-instruments inserted through incisions smaller than a centimeter, we can visualize, diagnose, and repair conditions within the knee, shoulder, hip, ankle, elbow, and wrist. This technique provides unparalleled visualization of joint structures while preserving surrounding healthy tissue. Our practice is equipped with the latest 4K arthroscopic systems and advanced instrumentation for the most precise surgical outcomes.
services.arthroscopyConditions: Torn meniscus and loose bodies in the knee; Shoulder impingement and rotator cuff tears; Labral tears in the hip and shoulder; Cartilage defects and chondral injuries; Ligament reconstruction of the knee and ankle; Synovitis and inflammatory joint conditions; Frozen shoulder (adhesive capsulitis); Elbow and wrist joint disorders
services.arthroscopyExpectations: After a thorough clinical evaluation and imaging review, we discuss whether arthroscopy is the optimal approach for your condition. The procedure is typically performed under regional anesthesia with sedation, lasting 30 minutes to 2 hours depending on complexity. Small portal incisions are made around the joint, through which the camera and instruments are inserted. Most arthroscopic procedures are performed as outpatient surgery, meaning you return home the same day with specific post-operative instructions and a rehabilitation plan.
services.arthroscopyRecovery: Arthroscopic surgery offers one of the fastest recovery timelines in orthopedics. Simple procedures like meniscus trimming allow return to daily activities within 1-2 weeks. More complex repairs, such as rotator cuff or labral reconstruction, require 2-4 weeks of protection followed by structured rehabilitation over 3-6 months. Physical therapy begins early to restore range of motion and strength. Most patients experience significantly less pain and scarring compared to traditional open surgery.
services.fractureCareDetail: Our fracture care program provides comprehensive treatment for all types of bone injuries, from simple stress fractures to complex multi-fragment and open fractures. We utilize the latest fixation technologies including anatomically contoured locking plates, intramedullary nails, and external fixation devices. Our approach prioritizes biological healing by preserving the blood supply to fractured bone while providing stable fixation that allows early mobilization. For complex cases, we employ 3D CT planning and intraoperative navigation to achieve anatomical reduction and optimal implant placement.
services.fractureCareConditions: Simple and compound fractures of the upper and lower extremities; Intra-articular fractures affecting joint surfaces; Pelvic and acetabular fractures; Open fractures requiring emergent treatment; Stress fractures from repetitive loading; Pathological fractures from osteoporosis or tumors; Malunions and nonunions from previous fractures; Periprosthetic fractures around joint implants
services.fractureCareExpectations: Fracture treatment begins with urgent stabilization and pain management. We obtain detailed imaging including X-rays and CT scans when needed to fully characterize the fracture pattern. Treatment options range from casting and bracing for stable fractures to surgical fixation for displaced or unstable injuries. When surgery is required, we discuss the specific technique, implant options, and expected timeline. Many fracture surgeries are performed within 24-48 hours of injury to optimize healing outcomes.
services.fractureCareRecovery: Recovery varies by fracture location and severity. Most simple fractures heal within 6-8 weeks, while complex fractures may require 3-4 months. Early protected motion begins within days of fixation to prevent joint stiffness. Weight-bearing is progressively advanced based on healing progress monitored through follow-up X-rays. Physical therapy focuses on restoring range of motion, strength, and function. We monitor healing closely and adjust the rehabilitation plan as bone consolidation progresses.
services.rehabilitationDetail: Our rehabilitation program is designed as an integral part of the treatment continuum, not an afterthought. We offer comprehensive pre-operative optimization (prehabilitation) and post-operative rehabilitation programs that are individually tailored to each patient's surgical procedure, physical condition, and personal goals. Our multidisciplinary approach combines physical therapy, occupational therapy, and patient education to accelerate recovery, minimize complications, and achieve the best possible functional outcomes. We utilize evidence-based protocols updated with the latest research.
services.rehabilitationConditions: Post-operative recovery from joint replacement surgery; Rehabilitation after ligament reconstruction and repair; Spine surgery recovery and core strengthening; Post-fracture rehabilitation and return to function; Chronic joint pain and degenerative conditions; Pre-surgical conditioning and optimization; Work-related musculoskeletal injuries; Balance and fall prevention for older adults
services.rehabilitationExpectations: Rehabilitation begins with a comprehensive functional assessment to establish baseline measurements and set realistic, measurable goals. Your personalized program may include manual therapy, therapeutic exercises, neuromuscular re-education, aquatic therapy, and modalities such as electrical stimulation and ultrasound. Sessions are typically scheduled 2-3 times per week, with a detailed home exercise program for the days between visits. We track progress using objective outcome measures and adjust your program as you advance through each recovery phase.
services.rehabilitationRecovery: The rehabilitation timeline is closely aligned with your surgical procedure and individual healing capacity. Phase 1 (weeks 1-2) focuses on pain management, swelling control, and gentle range of motion. Phase 2 (weeks 3-6) progresses to active strengthening and functional movements. Phase 3 (weeks 7-12) emphasizes advanced strengthening, balance training, and activity-specific exercises. Phase 4 (months 3-6) targets full return to work, sport, or daily activities with a maintenance program for long-term joint health.

detailSections.bookCta: Ready to discuss your treatment options?
